name: Tour de France Publicity Caravan - Safety and Timing Resource
summary: Official Tour resource explaining the publicity caravan and spectator safety rules.
whyItMatters: The caravan affects when to be in position before the riders and gives official safety guidance for every roadside viewing day.
pros: States the caravan precedes riders and describes a long continuous show
Includes spectator safety rules about staying off the road, not crossing, and watching for vehicles after the caravan
cons: Not stage-specific; exact passage times still need the stage timetable or caravan estimator
Does not list city-specific restrictions or Natura 2000 no-gift zones by stage

logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Relevant to all roadside viewing points
logistics.road_closure_notes: Use as safety context; road-closure timing must come from local authorities and stage schedules.

timing.best_visit_window: Be in position well before the caravan time shown on official stage timetables

sources.title: Tour de France - Follow the publicity caravan
sources.note: Official Tour page describing the caravan, its safety cars and vehicles, and spectator safety instructions.

sources.title: Tour de France - Stage pages
sources.note: Official route section links to stage timetables that include caravan passage estimates.

open_questions: For each selected viewing point, calculate caravan arrival from the official stage timetable.
Check whether mountain or protected-area sections restrict gift distribution.
